Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria), mild liver enzyme elevation (ALT), and low serum protein/globulin. Urinalysis reveals marked proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy with poor glycemic control and possible dehydration. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.
